hle.canonical_pass_notes: AGS Aging Cartridge results page showing MEMORY/LCD/TIMER/DMA/KEY INPUT/INTERRUPT all PASS, COM N/A. NBA is the LONE PASSER across all 4 runners - mgba and skyemu are stuck on AGS save-loader checksum screen, cable_club is stuck even earlier. The verification arc surfaced this as a major divergence and demonstrates why consensus-based scoring is wrong: mgba+skyemu agreement on the loader-error screen would have been recorded as 'gold' under the old scheme, masking the fact that they don't actually run the test suite at all.

cable_club.notes: Cable Club fails to run AGS - captures pure black screen. cable_club never reaches the AGS title or save-loader. Third real cable_club bug found by verification arc. See task #12.

mgba.notes: Stuck on AGS 'LOAD CONFIGURATION / CHECK SUM ERROR / DEFAULT IS SAVED' screen. AGS is trying to load saved configuration data and the runner doesn't provide a save file, so AGS shows the checksum error and never advances past the loader to actually run the tests. Bit-identical hash between mgba and skyemu - same shared failure mode. NOT a bug in either emulator strictly speaking; it's a runner-side test setup issue (we should provide AGS with a save file to skip the config loader). Filed as part of task #11 (test setup needs work).

nanoboyadvance.notes: LONE PASSER under HLE. NBA actually executes the AGS test suite to the results page: MEMORY PASS, LCD PASS, TIMER PASS, DMA PASS, COM N/A (requires link cable hardware), KEY INPUT PASS, INTERRUPT PASS. 6 of 7 sections pass on a single emulator instance.

Bit-identical hash between mgba and skyemu - same shared failure mode. NOT a bug in either emulator strictly speaking; it's a runner-side test setup issue (we should provide AGS with a save file to skip the config loader).
